summary: Updated the durable workshop website spec and operational current-status notes so repo documentation matches the homepage’s new Call for Papers language and current submission guidance.
work_completed: Reviewed the builder ResultPacket and current homepage content in `index.html`.
Updated `docs/product-specs/workshop-website.md` to replace the old placeholder-submissions description with the current Call for Papers behavior, navigation label, and remaining confirmed gaps.
Updated `.control-tower/docs/state/current-status.md` to reflect the live Call for Papers wording and the remaining unconfirmed submission workflow details.
Confirmed no new durable doc page or index refresh was needed because the existing workshop website spec already covered this surface area.

findings.message: Durable docs now match the homepage’s user-facing terminology: the site exposes a Program section and a Call for Papers section rather than a placeholder submissions area.

The homepage and docs still intentionally omit submission deadlines, destination links, and detailed formatting requirements beyond the current 4 to 6 page guidance because those details are not yet confirmed.

follow_up_recommendations: Update the Call for Papers copy and spec again once organizers confirm deadlines, formatting rules, and the submission destination.
Replace the provisional invited-talks/posters Program description once the workshop schedule and speaker lineup are finalized.
memory_worthy: Homepage copy now uses Call for Papers language and invites concise 4 to 6 page papers, while deadlines and submission destination links remain unconfirmed.
